expectations.evidence: Both login_screen.dart (class LoginScreen extends StatefulWidget) and register_screen.dart (class RegisterScreen extends StatefulWidget) exist as separate widget files with full form UIs including email, password fields, and navigation between each other.

auth_service.dart uses dart:io HttpClient (import 'dart:io'; final HttpClient _httpClient) instead of the Dio package. The summary explicitly states: 'dart:io HttpClient for network requests (no extra HTTP package dependency)'.

auth_provider.dart imports shared_preferences and implements _persistToken() which calls SharedPreferences.getInstance() and prefs.setString('auth_token', token), as well as tryAutoLogin() which reads the saved token and _clearPersistedToken() which removes it.

auth_wrapper.dart implements AuthWrapper widget that checks authProvider.isAuthenticated to decide whether to show the homeScreen or LoginScreen. It also calls tryAutoLogin() in initState to restore sessions on app start, and shows a loading spinner during initialization.

auth_provider.dart tracks _isLoading, _error, and success (return bool) states. Login/register methods use try/catch blocks handling AuthException (e.message) and generic exceptions ('An unexpected error occurred'). Both screens show SnackBar with error messages on failure and display CircularProgressIndicator during loading.
